name: Hexagonal Architecture (Ports & Adapters)

forces: Business logic must outlive frameworks, databases and delivery channels
The domain must be testable without infrastructure
Several adapters (HTTP, CLI, queue) over one core
x.structure: A domain core exposes ports (interfaces); adapters implement those ports for specific technologies. Every dependency points inward toward the domain (dependency inversion).
antiPatterns: Anemic domain with the real logic living in adapters/services
One port per database call, mirroring the ORM
Leaking framework or DB types across the port boundary
x.scale: Pays off in long-lived, multi-integration applications and services; over-engineered for simple CRUD or short-lived tools.

def: Isolates domain logic behind ports, with adapters connecting it to UI, DB and external systems.
problem: Keep business logic independent of frameworks, databases and delivery mechanisms.
mechanism: The domain defines ports (interfaces); adapters implement them for specific tech. Dependencies point inward toward the domain (dependency inversion).
failures: Anemic domain with logic in adapters
Port explosion mirroring every DB call
myths: "Clean/Onion/Hexagonal are different architectures" — same dependency-inversion idea, different framing
